conversations.value: Explain what a CPU cache is in simple terms.

A CPU cache is a very small and very fast memory inside the processor. It stores frequently used data so the CPU does not need to go to the much slower main memory (RAM) every time. Think of it like keeping tools on your desk instead of going to the garage every time you need them.

Machine learning is a method where computers learn patterns from data instead of being explicitly programmed with rules. After seeing enough examples, the computer can make predictions or decisions about new data.
The sky appears blue because air molecules scatter sunlight. Blue light is scattered more strongly than other colors, so we see blue when we look up during the day.
What does an operating system do?
An operating system manages the hardware of a computer and provides services to programs. It handles memory, files, processes, and lets applications run without needing to control hardware directly.
Recursion is when a function calls itself to solve smaller versions of the same problem. It continues until it reaches a base case where it stops.
